Hi, I'm Jose

I've worked in the quality space for over 20 years as a Senior Quality Engineer, Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET) and Quality Assurance Manager. In the ever evolving world of Software Development, the complexity of applications and the speed of delivery continue to increase. From a quality perspective, this typically means there is more to test, and less time to test it.

So how do we keep up? At times the solution is adopting new tools and frameworks. In some cases, it's about finding ways to integrate testing more closely into the development process, whether that's through shift-left testing, continuous integration, or other DevOps practices.

This blog is where I share practical write-ups on tools, frameworks and process adjustments I've found useful over the years.

MCP April 5, 2026

Implementing a Lightweight MCP Client in Node.js

Recently I was tasked with testing the functionality of a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. The goal was to validate the server's behavior when sending a query and receiving a response. After doing a bit of research, I decided to build a simple MCP client in Node.js.

Read more
Playwright April 2, 2026

Building a Playwright setup that stays readable as tests scale

A walkthrough of the project structure, fixtures, and baseline conventions that make Playwright suites easier to maintain when a team moves from a handful of tests to a real regression layer.

Read more
NodeJS March 18, 2026

API testing with Jest and Node.js without the brittle setup

A simple pattern for validating response contracts, organizing fixtures, and keeping your API test layer fast enough to run constantly during feature work.

Read more
API Design February 9, 2026

Using mock servers to unblock UI and API work in parallel

How mock services help teams validate edge cases earlier, reduce waiting on dependencies, and build more predictable test data for frontend and backend collaboration.

Read more
